text_initial: The one that I would most likely believe is the Roswell Crash  in 1947. Since I am now 78 years old, it is one theory that I have been aware of for a long time. I have read articles and viewed pictures from that event for many years and have been intrigued by the event. I am not sure that the pictures are all real and that the facts first published are accurate, but the way that the media first covered the story does bring many questions to mind. What is for sure is that something did crash in the desert that night in 1947, but what it was is still a mystery to me and I am still not certain of the government's explanation of these events. If there are no such things as flying saucers, why did the US government even suggest that explanation at the time. The headline in that New Mexico newspaper stated that a flying saucer was captured on that ranch. I do think there is a possibility that flying saucers do exist. Many videos are available on the internet showing pictures of night skies with unknown and unidentified objects visible in the sky. In the world today there are many explanations of these objects such as satellites, drones, etc., but the technology in 1947 was not that advanced . I need to review the book that was published years after the event to give me more information (or maybe convince me of "disinformation") concerning this theory.

text_initial: One of the latest conspiracy theories is that Donald Trump was actually recruited by the KGB and is a Russian operative known as Krasnov.  I find it interesting, considering his close ties with the Russian dictator, Vladimir Putin, and how he seems to admire the man.  It could explain why he is trying to lead our country in such an authoritarian way.  I hope this is just a conspiracy theory and has no truth to it though! A former Soviet intelligence officer, Alnur Mussayev, made this claim on Facebook recently.  He said that Trump was recruited in 1987.  He also said that Trump's file was being privately managed by one of Putin's close associates. Trump did visit Moscow in 1987 and the visit was overseen by Russian officials, which does make it a bit suspect.  We also know that in 1985, the KGB had a secret personality questionnaire that it distributed among its officers to show how to recognize and recruit Western figures.

text_initial: I would say, yes. I believe the paranormal, as well as the occult and other supernatural ideas, including mysterious creatures, are like conspiracy theories, in that they use the same meaning-making machinery we're built with. Meaning itself is "supernatural," it exists in the mind or consciousness, an inherently metaphysical no-thing. I find this compelling because most people like to shrug off all the supernatural woo-woo, or they buy into it as if biological werewolves were roaming the woods after having evolved over time. There's a nice middle. First, you have to spend a considerable amount of time getting to know all the major religions, or at least the biblical story very well. Then, you can begin to grasp the intuitive structure of meaning behind them. Once you've done this, you'll start to see this structure or "idos" (a fractal pattern) in everything, and that pattern is exactly what people pick up on when they dwell on their conspiracies or practice their science. My evidence is faith-based, which is to say, I have come to know the structure of reality personally and can only point in the direction of where you might go to cultivate your own relationship with it. I'm sorry if this sounds pompous, but I'm trying to be honest.

text_initial: One such conspiracy theory revolves around a series of timeline events that happened during my childhood and youth.  I was always interested in space and the moon landing as a child.  Later probes and spacecraft started being sent to Mars to get pictures and data, hoping to eventually land for more pictures, mapping, and samples.  Repeatedly these spacecraft would blow up on approach to Mars. Scientists reported a variety of reasons for failures and even the rare successes between the 70s and 90s.  But sometimes some of the scientists seemed to die mysteriously or from improbable circumstances.   It leant me to sometimes wonder if certain conspiracy theories about these events were not true or partially true.  These feelings came from conceivable facts and repetitive numbers pointing to something other than just 'accidents' or 'oversights'.   I think I shared most of the facts that I could see as a very young person that pointed to me thinking there was other information and persons behind the scenes not wanting to the space missions to succeed.

Later probes and spacecraft started being sent to Mars to get pictures and data, hoping to eventually land for more pictures, mapping, and samples. But the 70s were very challenging and it felt even as a young person entering middle school and highschool, that the push to defund Nasa and these scientists and projects was very palatable.  The push to move political focus and funds was intense.  It felt the more failures there were the more justification there was to defund the whole effort.  If there had been more success and provable positive outcomes of the missions, there would have been more political push to keep going.  There was not...I mean we didn't even have flights to Mars that were new or helpful in the 80s AT ALL.  As the internet came on line and research could be done, I tried to recreate the events I had experienced  but a lot of old newspapers weren't automatically loaded online and it is hard to find specifics sometimes.  That was frustrating.  As a kid, I was not clippings newspaper articles and saving them on this topic.  So there is 'how I remembered' things to be, which is always tricky.  But in 1988 I started to do some travels.  I travelled to Australia.  Had the great fortune as part of my travels to take the ride from one side of the country to the other mainly using Australia Hwy 1.   Laster when I got home there was a news story about a Nasa scientist working on one of the sat dishes there that we helped Australia put together. He was helping align something for the then next Mars flyby.   Regardless the article told of this scientist dying because he accidently hit a tree on Hwy 1 when travelling back to the east coast.   I tell you there are no trees there....so it sparked my feelings again. Too me it still felt like political forces at work from time to time to shift the tides of money, power, and popular thinking to their liking.  Happily many who love space and see it's future progress as human progress have continued to fight around these issues and slooowwwly move forward.  Meanwhile, I may be dead before we get to Mars now.  Super bummer. ||| I agree.  The journey is long and difficult but worth it.  Others of not a similar mindset whom purposefully or not set forth road blocks really stinks.  I hope we make it. Thank you for the conversation.

text_initial: Although I don't follow any particular religion I do feel that we are all connected here on Earth. I feel that since energy can neither be created nor destroyed, and that the soul is energy, that it continues to exist after death. I can't say what happens after we die but I feel as if the spirit cannot be destroyed by death. This theory would probably be refuted by scientists but it's something that I believe. I can give you examples of why I feel this theory is true. I have a friend who lost a child shortly after the baby was born. Everyone was in mourning for a while. One day her older child told her that she shouldn't feel sad because there was another baby boy in her belly and her son told her his name. She though that was such a sweet thing for him to wish for but she didn't believe him right away. A couple of days later she was feeling off and she remembered what her son told her. She went out and bought an over the counter pregnancy test and sure enough it was positive. Several months later she delivered a healthy baby boy and they named him what her son said his name was. It's things like this that make me know that there's more to living than what scientists can see, hear, feel, theorize and test for. The bounds of the spirit's persistence after death conform to the laws of conservation of energy--at least as we know that law today.

The memories, the behaviors--everything that makes us who we are is controlled by the brain and its electrical pathways. So it's not entirely accurate to say that the spirit or soul isn't rooted in the physical. Metaphysics involves a great many different phenomena but you can't deny the fact that we are energy and our spirits are energy as well, can you? ||| I appreciate the discussion even though I may disagree with some of your points. text_initial: I do hold religious beliefs that many around me would disagree with; I believe that it is not wrong before the eyes of God for two people of the same gender to love one another romantically. I know that many experts in biblical studies argue that there are specific passages in the Bible forbidding this. I have read those passages, but I still do not agree. I believe those passages were written for different times, and I do not at all believe that God would condemn anyone for feeling or expressing true, selfless love, regardless of who they feel it for. I have met same-sex couples before in person who seem to have a good, loving relationship with one another and are generally kind, hardworking people. They are the type of people that the Bible encourages people to be. This led me to believe that God would certainly not punish people like that.

text_initial: One conspiracy theory is depopulation. The Georgia Guidestones states 10 rules to guide humanity. The one I remember most is to maintain a world population at 500 million people. In order for that to be true, 7.5 billion of us have to go. I don't know how true this is. I do know that our global leaders know more than they are letting on. I know that elected officials, such as senators, representatives, and governors know about this too, and they get very uncomfortable when people start asking questions. The most compelling experiences I had was talking to someone (I cannot mention names) who has a top secret security clearance. In a round about manner, this person validated many of my suspicions in a round about manner, without leaking too much information. This person was someone I've known for a long time. Then I also watched similar videos on YouTube, where other experts give accounts that are nearly identical to the person I spoke with.

I am not sure this is borne out with actual psychological testing. My knee-jerk reaction upon hearing a conspiracy theory (flat earth, fake moon landing, astrology) is to consider it stupid, and I apply that to its supporters. I am open to changing my opinion upon learning a new fact, but the "evidence" the conspiracy theory people share are NOT facts.

text_initial: I believe there is something serious about the number of freeway wrecks soon after a National Guard unit returns from war.  In just a few weeks I will notice a surprising number of people who die on the freeway, going the wrong way and crashing into an 18-wheeler.  I've done a little homework on this, and I see that MANY of them were members of a recently returned National Guard unit.  They know if they just commit suicide, the life insurance may not even pay a dime, but if they get killed in an accident, there is usually double indemnity.  Head-first into a Freightliner is a quick way to do it, and probably painless at 75 mph.  And the wife and kids get that double indemnity, and the bad, nasty dreams will strop.  I may be wrong, but I do see the number of such wrecks go up when the troops come home.   I just noticed it a while back, when guys were coming home from Afghanistan.  The number of wrong-way wrecks on the freeway jumped.  The people in charge blamed it on the "WRONG WAY" signs and several other things, but the number went up right when these guys were coming home.  I also remember when Korea ended, many vets committed suicide, and many did after and during Viet Nam.  I'm a disabled vet myself, and I have seen war, and I have experienced coming home from war.  I KNOW I was luckier than others.  I have seen many that came back "changed".  For some, you just can't put it back together the way it was.  And this world they came back to is totally uncontrollable now.  It is horribly sad, indeed, but for some, there must be a way to make the dreams stop, and they have to hurt as few as possible.  ---Better to die in an accident than die as a crazy man.

It is horribly sad, indeed, but for some, there must be a way to make the dreams stop, and they have to hurt as few as possible.  ---Better to die in an accident than die as a crazy man. ||| As for #1, we can't distinguish an accident from a suicide in these cases, but we must look at the possibility, and NOT with the help of the insurance companies.  They will call ALL of then suicide, just to save money.  As for #2, as a kid, I remember several cases I read in the paper of someone who accidently shot himself while cleaning a rifle.  The Obit would note the guy was a returning vet.  Every hunter and every vet knows to empty a gun before you clean it.  As for #3, you have to understand that a person in this condition only knows that his whole life is nuts.  He believes if he tells everyone he is nuts, they will just put him in a rubber room and that will be the end of it.  He will still have the dreams, but now there will be no help at all, and he has lost everything.  ---At least that is what he believes, because that is all he has ever seen about 'mental health'.   As for #4, that is why if anyone does any investigating on this theory, the insurance companies will drop policies on vets, because they are a business.  As for #5, we have had the discussions.  These people do NOT want the VA or the Community or anyone else "helping" them.  We are the ones who sent them to that god-awful place and screwed up their life. They have had enough of our 'support'.  Even more, they sure can't stand up and say, "Look at me!"   They are having enough problems at home and trying to get a job.  You have to understand these were normal people when they left, and their brain does not want them to tell ANYONE that they are not normal now.  And as for the convenience of mental health or any other facilities, the current president is starting to pull the plug on the VA.  ---After all, just like the post office, it does not turn a profit.  I agree we need a better environment, but it won't happen by accident.  And as long as we think that mental health is a horrible thing to discuss, we will be unable to solve this.  Congress will never bring it up.  Actually, it is a bigger problem to solve, since it isn't just mental health that we do not want to work on.  Physical help is still a huge problem.  Tomorrow, wherever you go, take note of what you see.  Do you actually see handicapped entrances?  Are they functional?  Take a walk through a VA hospital, then ask yourself if you ever see these people on the street?  No, you don't.   I agree we should foster 'a more supportive environment', but I don't know how.  Neither does anyone in the government.  These vets can have 'discussion sessions' all day long, but that won't solve anything. ||| Thank you for listening.  I sincerely appreciate it.  Now if only we could get the government to listen...   Again, Thanks.

text_initial: I find a wide view of religion to be favorable to myself. The theory of "one correct religion" feels incorrect to me. I prefer a broad view, where every religion, and every deity has some validity. When you take away the human element, boiling away corruptions and selfishness, most religion becomes a guiding principal of "be a good person." Many might disagree with this, due to their own personal beliefs. I was raised Catholic, but early in life, began to question the principals, requirements, and obligations. As I grew, I cautiously explored other religions, and found that one I looked past most of the ceremony, requirements, etc., they all seemed to come down to the same basic guiding principal, the golden rule, as it were. Be a good person, treat others as you want to be treated.

text_initial: The most compelling one which stands out in my mind is the assasination of President John F. Kennedy. The Zapruder film showed Kennedy's head snap back during the second shot. Also, tthe pieces of his skull and brain all went flying backwards and I've read many books and have watched many movies and documentaries which raise question to the lone assasin theory. Kennedy was responsible for the Bay of Pigs fiasco which the CIA originally planned but Kennedy pulled his military support at the last minute. Also, his brother Bobby, who was the U.S. attorney general at the time, was zeroing in on the mafia and they may have ordered the hit. One more thing is that Jack Ruby, who assasinated Lee Harvey Oswald in the Texas jail as he was being transfered, was put in prison in Texas and, despite his pleas to go to Washington to testify, was never allowed to do that. These were all things which should have been investigated more thoroughly befor the witnesses all died.

Additionally, democrat politicians as recent as President Obama were against illegal immigration.  Even President Biden, when he was a senator, was on record against illegal immigration. The previous stance by top democrats on illegal immigration versus the past four years of unjustified record numbers of border crossing and manipulation of the asylum system.
text_all:
The Democrat Party is trying to change the democrat voting base through mass illegal immigration.  There are many recorded high level party members stating this.  Additionally, democrat politicians as recent as President Obama were against illegal immigration.  Even President Biden, when he was a senator, was on record against illegal immigration. The previous stance by top democrats on illegal immigration versus the past four years of unjustified record numbers of border crossing and manipulation of the asylum system. ||| The situation is not more complex.  It’s a simple long term goal of retaining power by single party rule. If immigration reform were the actual goal then President Obama would’ve passed immigration reform during his first 2 years in office while the Democrats had control of the House and Senate.  Democrats, and Republicans appear to want immigration reform to remain a wedge issue for attacking each other during political campaigns.  However, with recent shifts in polling showing certain demographics voting more republican, the democrats have resorted to radical immigration influxes with massive tax payer funding going to support the majority of the migrants flowing into the country. By completely overloading the immigration system, at some point there will be an opportunity to pass an amnesty bill that will give a large portion of the migrants legal citizenship. In turn, the newest wave of migrants will vote for the party that “let them come”.  This is not a short term goal because over 90% of asylum claims are denied. If the immigration court system is so overburdened, there will be a push to grant illegal immigrants citizenship because they’ve already established themselves and likely had children, and integrated into the country.  In specific response to #3; the record number of recent border crossings were not influenced by a multitude of factors because those factors have existed for decades. The single factor that did create the incentive for mass groups of thousands of migrants to surge across the border was President Biden and his administration encouraging them to come. It was no coincidence that there were large groups coming to the border wearing shirts that said “Biden let us in”. ||| 1. President Obama passed the Affordable Care Act along party lines and could’ve done the same with immigration reform, which, by the way, has never been defined by either party. It’s a broad blanket statement used by politicians while campaigning. The US has immigration laws in place already that allow for legal migration.
2: Both parties regularly monitor voting demographics by many factors including race, gender, age, etc. While no race should be considered a monolithic voting block, it is widely known, and shown through many voting cycles, that certain races vote mostly Democrat, such as the African American vote (as an example). Voting trends by race cannot be just conveniently ignored or glossed over by the statement that they are not a monolithic voting block when in every election cycle polls show these groups vote mostly the same.
3: In the last 4 years there were record numbers of border crossings. The primary reason is obvious, and shown through actual recorded interviews with migrants that they are coming for opportunity, whether it is financial, or for the welfare and healthcare. The Biden administration created the incentive and possibly a once in a lifetime opportunity for them to risk their lives to attempt a dangerous journey to get to the border. Again, most asylum claims are denied and economic or climate changes are not reasons to be granted asylum.  A more nefarious and underlying reason, in addition to a potential future Democrat voting block, exists when possibly the most powerful Democrat Politician in history, Nancy Pelosi, said who else is going to pick our fruit?
4: Yes, the system to attain legal status in most cases is long and burdensome.  Why was the 1986 IRCA passed by President Reagan? Possibly in hopes of unburdening an overburdened system then? This past act is a good predictor of a future goal.  The so called “ Dreamers”, created by executive fiat, are an example of this. It is not likely this will ever become a bipartisan issue again.
5: People can see with their own eyes what message was being delivered to the migrant caravans.  Somebody paid thousands of dollars for those T-shirts to be made and it surely wasn’t the migrants wearing them.  It doesn’t take a college graduate to understand that.  

Lastly, constantly downplaying the significance of the massive surge of illegal immigration as a nuanced and complex issue, and not a political or nefarious purpose, by implying people are not sophisticated enough to comprehend the matter is arrogant and naive.  The reasons are obvious to even the most unsophisticated of society.

text_initial: Does the JFK assassination still count? It's been confirmed now that a secret service agent guarding the president fired the fatal shot, and that's been my opinion on the matter since I first heard theories as a child. It's the only thing that made sense relating how poorly the government was hiding that something with wrong. If our own government had tried to kill the president, it would have been better planned. All the coverup came literally after the fact, which points to the government trying to hide what would look like incompetence. The truth is that JFK died to unfortunate circumstances that were set into motion by Lee Harvey Oswald, therefore LHO is the one responsible. The mystery here itself was compelling for so long due to how obvious it was and how ridiculous the actual government response was at the time. The magic bullet theory was so utterly bullshit that it made the whole case remain in public awareness for more than half a century. This was the best the government could do at the time? They would have been better off leaning in on a second gunman on the grassy knoll, but they couldn't because then the gunman would either "get away with it" when he wasn't found, or they would have to ruin someone else's life who wasn't even involved, like they already did with pushing Jack Ruby into murdering Oswald to avoid having everything come out in court.
text_all: Does the JFK assassination still count? It's the only thing that made sense relating how poorly the government was hiding that something with wrong. If our own government had tried to kill the president, it would have been better planned. All the coverup came literally after the fact, which points to the government trying to hide what would look like incompetence. The truth is that JFK died to unfortunate circumstances that were set into motion by Lee Harvey Oswald, therefore LHO is the one responsible. The mystery here itself was compelling for so long due to how obvious it was and how ridiculous the actual government response was at the time. The magic bullet theory was so utterly bullshit that it made the whole case remain in public awareness for more than half a century. This was the best the government could do at the time? They would have been better off leaning in on a second gunman on the grassy knoll, but they couldn't because then the gunman would either "get away with it" when he wasn't found, or they would have to ruin someone else's life who wasn't even involved, like they already did with pushing Jack Ruby into murdering Oswald to avoid having everything come out in court. ||| Now that government documents have been released with a better and more thorough explanation, the mystery is essentially solved.  A secret service agent fired the fatal shot, but Oswald was working alone and was responsible for the chain of events that occurred. Other theories wouldn't have gained any traction at all if the government had not been trying to protect this agent and itself from scrutiny. It's poor response in the moment to try to back it's claims with science, particularly concerning Oswald's actual skill with a rifle to land multiple shots and the injuries being inconsistent with shots fired from behind, is why conspiracy theories never died over time. It was clear that the government answer was the wrong one. The actual effects of the coverup are why people don't trust their government today decades later. Because we have actual proof the government will lie to protect itself, even when, transparency would have been the better answer, because what actually happened was understandable. ||| I have nothing further to say on this matter.

text_initial: That COVID was developed in a lab in China; I subscribe to this theory based on the descriptions of the perfection of the protein spikes on the COVID molecule. It did not evolve that way, it came out of China that way.  While the virus has mutated and adapted along the way, there's not getting past the reality that the earliest forms of the virus are uniform.  It was designed that way in the experiments. ||| I think you said it best with "virologists and evolutionary biologists have mapped the changes and mutations in SARS-CoV-2 as it has spread."  Mapped the changes and mutations as it spread - we have never, ever seen the lab reports out of China.  Not ever.  We have no idea what this virus looked like on day one.  No one does outside of China.  The first first form of the virus we saw in the first infected person had uniform protein spikes.  It did not evolve over time with these spikes.  It came with them.  I think the biggest issue with the narrative around COVID lies in the reality that we will never have the initial information into the research being done with the virus prior to the outbreak.  As long as that secrecy remains, many of us will speculate about its origins.  It's just our nature as human beings.

text_initial: I don't usually buy into mainstream conspiracy theories (moon landing, etc.), but I do believe there are truths that have not been shared with the public, especially when those truths involve powerful people such as politicians.  One example is Jackie Onassis' belief that Lyndon Johnson and others were behind JFK's assassination.  I find it compelling because it could actually be true, whether it was Lyndon Johnson or whether it was others, there could have been a political motivation to his assassination.  However, after so much investigation, I'm inclined to believe Lee Harvey Oswald acted alone. The fact that Jackie Onassis, with all her power and influence, believed it is what made it a more legitimate possibility to me.

text_initial: I believe that countries should have open borders which many experts believe would be unmanageable. I don't think people should have to suffer because of where they were born. It may be logistically difficult, but I think it could lead to changes in the world if people were able to leave bad situations. It could influence governments to have more civil rights or risk losing their population.  I have read statistics that suggest that immigrants (to the US in this case) commit less crimes and are more likely to start small businesses. I believe that having diversity, and communities from many cultures increases awareness and teaches me to think differently about my own beliefs. It helps me identify biases.
